author: Thomas W. Brink, MD
executive_summary: Dr. Brink, a retail investor, vehemently opposes a proposed SEC rule that would allow corporations to shift from quarterly to semi-annual financial reporting. He argues this change would create an informational asymmetry that benefits corporate insiders and wealthy elites at the expense of ordinary individual investors.

positions.transparency: strongly supports maintaining quarterly reporting transparency; opposes any reduction in disclosure frequency

economic_arguments: Reducing reporting frequency from quarterly to semi-annual creates informational advantages for corporate insiders
Insiders would be able to anticipate market reactions to financial results and trade profitably before public disclosure
The rule disproportionately benefits wealthy and elite market participants at the expense of retail investors
competition_arguments: Semi-annual reporting would create an uneven playing field between insiders with access to non-public information and ordinary retail investors
The rule is characterized as favoring elite market participants over average individual investors
retail_investor_arguments: Individual investors rely on frequent financial disclosures to make informed investment decisions
Reducing reporting frequency harms retail investors by widening the information gap between them and corporate insiders
The proposed change is viewed as fundamentally unfair to ordinary investors
recommended_actions: Reject the proposed rule to allow semi-annual financial reporting
Maintain the existing quarterly financial reporting requirement for publicly traded corporations
key_quotes: I write to vehemently oppose the proposed regulation that would allow corporations to report financial information every six months instead of every three months.
This change will allow insiders to determine how the markets will perceive corporations and profit from the knowledge that they have to the exclusion of individual investors.
In other words, 'The rich get richer' and the poor man suffers.
This proposed rule is purely for the elite.
